channel.title: JLT What's Trending in Travel
channel.description: What's Trending in Travel — weekly UK travel trends for JLT agents

item.title: UK families aren't cancelling summer. They're date-shifting hard. ✈️
item.description:
UK families aren't cancelling summer. They're date-shifting hard. ✈️
The latest YouGov data backs up what so many travel agents are seeing right now:
- 31% of UK travellers are going off-peak. Late August and early September are the sweet spot.
- 19% are swapping abroad for a UK break. No flights to price-watch, no currency stress.
- 71% are considering a mountain or lake escape this summer or autumn. 🏔️
- Multi-gen trips are growing. Villas and clustered cruise cabins for 6-12 people.
The agent who makes the value obvious wins the booking. This week's blog breaks it all down.
Full blog on the website… link in the comments 🔗
First comment:
https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/whats-trending-in-travel-smart-value-summer-2026-06-25

item.title: UK travellers are not pulling back… they are getting smarter about when and how they spend. 🌍
item.description:
UK travellers are not pulling back… they are getting smarter about when and how they spend. 🌍
Four trends are pointing the same direction this week:
- 42% of travellers are booking earlier to lock in better timing (Criteo) - Coolcation demand is rising fast… Scandinavia bookings tipped to grow up to 35% this year - Clients are consolidating into one longer, more meaningful holiday rather than three short breaks - Travellers browse 25 hotel listings on average before deciding… your follow-up structure matters
The big opportunity? Help clients be clever with their timing and their budget. That is where trust is built. ✈️
Full blog on the website… link in the comments 🔗

item.title: TEST — What's Trending Facebook feed check (safe to delete)
item.description: This is a test item used to register the What's Trending Facebook feed in Sociamonials. It carries no real content and is safe to delete once the RSS source has been added. The first real post will replace it on the next What's Trending run.
